title: hebe hectorii subsp. demissa
intro: Hebe hectorii subsp. demissa is a sub-species of the Hebe hectorii plant, also known as a New Zealand native shrub that belongs to the Veronica family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The health benefits of Hebe hectorii subsp. demissa include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Hebe hectorii subsp. demissa is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
